prose:
Benzene frontier-orbital (HOMO–LUMO gap) calculation via DFT, tier standard (B3LYP/def2-TZVP), gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Geometry: user-supplied benzene.xyz (C₆H₆, 12 atoms). Calculation performed with PySCF (RKS, exact four-center integrals, no density fitting). SCF converged in 8 cycles. HOMO energy = −7.0468 eV; LUMO energy = −0.4157 eV; **HOMO–LUMO gap = 6.6311 eV** (Koopmans-style orbital gap; note: this is not a true excitation energy, which would require TDDFT). Benzene's HOMO and LUMO are each near-degenerate pairs reflecting π-system symmetry (HOMO-1 and HOMO differ by <0.001 eV; LUMO and LUMO+1 by ~0.0006 eV). All integrity checks passed; calculation is trustworthy. Command: `cli frontier --method dft --tier standard --charge 0 --mult 1 --accept-defaults /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/benzene/benzene.xyz`. Output: `/home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpafwujqaq/live.json`.</prose>
<parameter name="integrity_trustworthy">true
